name: Set Up TypeScript ESLint with Type-Aware Rules
description: Configure ESLint with typescript-eslint for type-aware linting rules that catch bugs the compiler misses, including no-floating-promises and consistent-type-imports.
trendingReason: typescript-eslint v8 flat config is now the standard for TypeScript linting

steps.title: Install typescript-eslint
steps.description: Add ESLint and typescript-eslint packages for flat config support.

steps.title: Create eslint.config.mjs with type-aware rules
steps.description: Set up a flat config that enables type-checked linting rules.
steps.tip: Use strictTypeChecked for maximum safety. Fall back to recommendedTypeChecked if strict has too many errors initially.

steps.title: Run the linter
steps.description: Execute ESLint across all TypeScript files and review errors.

steps.title: Add a lint script and fix command
steps.description: Add convenient npm scripts for linting.

faq.question: What are type-aware rules and why do they matter?
faq.answer: Type-aware rules use the TypeScript compiler's type information during linting. They catch issues like unhandled promises (no-floating-promises), unsafe any usage, and incorrect type assertions that regular ESLint rules cannot detect.

faq.question: Is type-aware linting slow?
faq.answer: It is slower than syntax-only linting because it requires type-checking. Use projectService: true (typescript-eslint v8+) for faster performance. For large projects, consider running type-aware rules only in CI and using faster syntax rules locally.

faq.question: How do I migrate from .eslintrc to flat config?
faq.answer: Create eslint.config.mjs and move your rules there. Replace extends with imported configs, replace env/globals with languageOptions, and use the ignores property instead of .eslintignore. The typescript-eslint docs have a detailed migration guide.
